grant_title: Juvenile Justice Commission Summer Programs for At-Risk Youth Grants
project_area: Summer programs for at-risk youth focusing on recreational, educational, and character-building activities, including job training, mental health and substance abuse treatment, community-based programs, reentry/aftercare services, and school programs to prevent truancy.
demographic_requirements: At-risk youth in New Jersey, with a focus on vulnerable populations such as those from low-income communities, court-involved youth, and youth with behavioral or mental health challenges.
eligibility_summary: Organizations operating successful summer programs for at-risk youth in New Jersey are eligible to apply. Applicants must demonstrate a track record of serving at-risk youth and submit a competitive application reviewed by the Juvenile Justice Commission (JJC) and the Governor’s Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention (JJDP) Committee. Grants range from $7,067 to $30,000, with a total of 17 grants awarded.
